albums.title: Songs From The Seaside
albums.description:
LONGY AND THE GOSPEL TRASH
Liner Notes for Songs From The Seaside – Live in Southend”

There are live albums, and then there are documents. This is the latter.

It’s Songs From The Seaside Live in Southend captures Longy and the Gospel Trash at full tilt: 11 songs, a full big band, an upstairs pub in Southend, and a room thick with sweat, brass, electricity and belief. No studio polish. No safety net. Just wood floors, warm beer, overblown horns, and songs that have already lived a few lives before landing here.

Recorded in Longy’s hometown, upstairs in a pub where the ceiling feels low enough to lean on and the stage was never designed to hold that many players, this album isn’t about perfection. It’s about presence. You can hear the crowd pressed in close. You can hear glasses clink between verses. You can hear the band pushing air around the room like they’re trying to lift the roof clean off.

Longy and the Gospel Trash have always existed somewhere between sermon and bar fight. There’s gospel in the uplift, in the call-and-response spirit, in the sense that every chorus is meant to be sung by more than just the person holding the microphone. And there’s trash in the glorious refusal to tidy things up — in the ragged edges, the overdriven amps, the horn lines that sway instead of march.

For this recording, the songs were rebuilt for a big band setting. Brass punches through choruses like sunlight through pub windows at last orders. The rhythm section swings when it wants to, stomps when it needs to, and occasionally threatens to derail entirely before snapping back into place. It’s live music in its most honest form: slightly dangerous, deeply communal, completely unrepeatable.

These twelve songs feel less like a setlist and more like a reckoning. They carry hometown ghosts. They carry road miles. They carry laughter, stubborn hope, and a refusal to let small rooms shrink big feelings. If you’ve ever stood shoulder-to-shoulder with strangers and shouted a chorus back at a band because it felt necessary, you already know what this record is about.

Southend isn’t just the location — it’s part of the instrumentation. The salt air, the arcades, the stubbornness of a seaside town that keeps reinventing itself. Recording upstairs in a pub wasn’t a nostalgic choice. It was a statement: the songs belong to rooms like this. They belong to communities that show up.

And show up they did.

Published into the Valueverse

This album is published by the New Music Nudge Unit into the Valueverse — because how music is shared matters just as much as how it’s made.

The Valueverse is a growing digital ecosystem built around direct support, open networks, and artist sovereignty. Instead of relying on traditional streaming models where value trickles down through opaque systems, the Valueverse is designed around transparency, participation, and direct connection.

At the heart of it is V4V — Value for Value.

Value for Value is simple: if something moves you, you return value in whatever form you can. That might be money, time, attention, skills, amplification, or community. There are no paywalls forcing you in and no algorithms deciding what’s worthy. It’s voluntary, relational, and human.

Listeners become participants. Artists become nodes in a network rather than products in a catalogue. Support flows directly. Appreciation becomes tangible. Culture becomes collaborative again.

#demu
#v4v

This live album isn’t just a recording of twelve songs in an upstairs pub. It’s part of a wider movement experimenting with how art can circulate without losing its soul.

albums.title: Healing Hands
albums.description:
Healing Hands” enters the Valueverse as a quiet anthem of restoration and presence. With warmth and restraint, Longy delivers a song about the steady power of care — the kind that doesn’t shout, but lands.

Longy — the international musical street brawler from Southend-on-Sea, Essex — brings grit and soul in equal measure, blending lived experience with melodic honesty.

Released across open rails like Nostr, the track reflects a new era of independent music: direct, peer-to-peer, and powered by listeners who carry it forward.

Published by the New Music Nudge Unit.

albums.title: Lady, You Shot Me!
albums.description:
Longy steps into the Valueverse with a soulful cover of Sam Cooke’s “Lady, You Shot Me.”
A timeless song, reinterpreted with respect for the original and a sound shaped by today’s independent music rails. 

 

If you feel this one, boost it, zap it, and share it forward.
Music lives when people pass it hand to hand.

🎶 Original by Sam Cooke
🎤 Cover performed by Longy
⚡ Released into the Valueverse

#Valueverse #SamCooke #LadyYouShotMe #CoverSong #IndependentMusic 
#ZapTheMusic

This release is part of a wider move toward decentralising music through the Valueverse — a listener-supported ecosystem where fans directly support independent artists via value-for-value platforms.

Listen & support via the Valueverse:
• Fountain
• Podverse
• Other value-enabled apps

More music & links:
https://bitcoinessex.co.uk/new-music-nudge-unit/

albums.title: Live at the Fickle Pickle - Into the Valueverse
albums.description:
This is the live album exclusive connected to the show - Into the Valueverse - Remember this is a value for value album and the artists have put it out for free, so if you enjoyed this record? please send them some value back with a message and tell them who you are and where you are listening from. Bitcoin prefered! All of the artists and crew are #V4V enabled and almost all the value sent will go directly to them. 

Presented by the New Music Nudge Unit, Into the Valueverse was a groundbreaking live music event held on Friday, 23rd May 2025 at The Fickle Pickle Club in Westcliff-on-Sea, Essex.

As the UK’s first-ever Bitcoin-powered gig, it fused cutting-edge technology with raw, independent sound in an unforgettable night of borderless, peer-to-peer performance.

Live Stream Sound & Video: @iNPC

Sound Engineer for the room: Joe Lamb

Host and Exec Producer: Aaron Dorn (Aaron of Essex)

🎤 Lineup:

Joe Martin – Americana storyteller with soul

Setlist:
1. Checkmate
2. My Heart Decided For Me
3. High Gravity
4. The Jetsons
5. Crocodile Tears
6. Empty Passenger Seat


Nathan Abbott – Bluesy Essex heart with a rock edge
Drums: Archie
Bass: Andy Carr

Setlist:
1. The Big I Am
2. Jenny
3. Into the Light
4. Upside Down
5. Looking Glass
6. 50 - 1
7. Sweet Sunshine

Longy – Southend rebel with electrifying punk-folk fire
Drums - @Tommydrums

Set list:
1. Heroes/Tape Up
2. Love Schizophrenia
3. Promise to my people
4. Back from the Abyss
5. Penthouses and Pavements
6. Careful What You Wish For 
7. Don't get me Started


To access the valueverse download a modern podcast app such as https://Fountain.fm or listen in via your browser using https://lnbeats.com or https://curiocaster.com

The valueverse is where artists publish their music to the Bitcoin layer of the internet, allowing fans and listeners to send “boosts” (small Bitcoin payments) directly to them. It’s music in its purest form: peer-to-peer, value-for-value. Artists receive almost all of the value, with no middlemen — just music, connection, and freedom. If you want to know more then message aaron @ newmusicnudgeunit@gmail.com

All photography provided by the awesome Raj - https://www.rajeshtaylor.com/into-the-valueverse-fickle-pickle-club-westcliff-on-sea 

Show was supported by the legends at https://AZTE.co - Get your bitcoin in seconds!
https://fountain.fm - The podcast app which is revolutionising how we listen.

https://bitcoinessex.co.uk - Getting everyone in the room!
https://headstarts.uk - Making the idea become a reality.

albums.title: Into the Valueverse - Full Show
albums.description:
Presented by the New Music Nudge Unit, Into the Valueverse was a groundbreaking live music event held on Friday, 23rd May 2025 at The Fickle Pickle Club in Westcliff-on-Sea, Essex.
As the UK’s first-ever Bitcoin-powered gig, it fused cutting-edge technology with raw, independent sound in an unforgettable night of borderless, peer-to-peer performance.

Live Stream Sound & Video: @iNPC

Sound Engineer for the room: Joe Lamb

Host and Exec Producer: Aaron Dorn (Aaron of Essex)

🎤 Lineup:

Longy – Southend rebel with electrifying punk-folk fire
Drums - @Tommydrums

Joe Martin – Americana storyteller with soul

Nathan Abbott – Bluesy Essex heart with a rock edge
Drums: Archie
Bass: Andy Carr


Joe Martin's Set = time stamp - 00:05:00 - 00:35:00

1. Checkmate
2. My Heart Decided For Me
3. High Gravity
4. The Jetsons
5. Crocodile Tears
6. Empty Passenger Seat

Aaron's Rant = 00:35:13 - 00:38:20

Nathan Abbott's Set = time stamp - 00:38:40 - 01:09:29

1. The Big I Am
2. Jenny
3. Into the Light
4. Upside Down
5. Looking Glass
6. 50 - 1
7. Sweet Sunshine

Booster Bowie Re-cap = time stamp - 01:09:33 - 01:16:15

Longy's Set = time stamp - 01:16:16 - 01:53:50

1. Heroes/Tape Up
2. Love Schizophrenia
3. Promise to my people
4. Back from the Abyss
5. You Shine
6. Careful What You Wish For 
7. Don't get me Started


The show ran on Bitcoin’s value-for-value model, allowing fans to send real-time support — boosts and zaps — directly to the artists during their sets. It was livestreamed globally via Podcasting 2.0 apps and NOSTR Live, making it accessible to anyone, anywhere.

We raised over 0.0045 BTC (around £375 at the time) — all routed directly to the artists with no middlemen, no gatekeepers.

This recording captures the live show exactly as it happened, complete with value time splits — so when the artist changes, the bitcoin flows to the right wallet, seamlessly in the background.

All photos by https://www.rajeshtaylor.com/into-the-valueverse-fickle-pickle-club-westcliff-on-sea

🙏 Powered by:
Fountain.fm, Azte.co, RSS Blue, The Fickle Pickle, Bitcoin Essex, iNPC, and Headstarts.uk.

⚡ Music met money — and the crowd made it matter.
